LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS AEG LIVE, LLC AND ANSCHUTZ 12K COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANTS AEG AND ANSCHUTZ. LABOR CODE SECTION 3602(D) BARS CLAIM AGAINST AEG. LACK OF ACTIVE PARTICIPATION AND HAVING ONLY GENERAL CONTROL OF THE RACE IS INSUFFICIENT TO CREATE LIABILITY AS TO ANSCHULZ. SEE HOOKER VS. DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION (2002) 27 CAL. 4TH 198. COURT OVERRULED ALL OBJECTIONS. COURT DENIED INTERVENOR'S JOINDER IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE HOWARD COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE ISSUES IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER DWIGHT ACTED AS HOWARD'S AGENT/EMPLOYEE AND BREACHED HIS DUTY TO PROVIDE A SAFE ENVIRONMENT. COURT SUSTAINED OBJECTIONS NUMBERS 5, 6 AND 7 AS TO THE JASINSKI DECLARATION BUT OVERRULED ALL THE REMAINING OBJECTIONS AS TO BOTH SIDES. COURT DENIED INTERVENOR'S JOINDER IN PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Defendant Aeg Live, Llc And Anschutz 12k Company Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS AEG LIVE, LLC AND ANSCHUTZ 12K COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
APPEARANCES: Tad S. Shapiro, Esq. of Shapiro, Galvin, Shapiro & Moran 707/544-5858 for pltf & opposing party; Ramsey F. Kawar, Esq. of Low, Ball & Lynch 981-6630 for defts & moving parties AEG LIVE, LLC AND ANSCHUTZ 12K COMPANY; Jill K. Rizzo, Esq. of LeClairRyan 913-4901 for deft The Howard Company; David W. Hughes, Esq. of Laughlin Falbo Levy & Moresi LLP 510/622-5332 for Intervenor American Home Assurance Company.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for Summary Judgment granted as to defendants AEG and Anschutz. Labor Code section 3602(d) bars claim against AEG. Lack of active participation and having only general control of the race is insufficient to create liability as to Anschulz. See Hooker vs. Department of Transportation (2002) 27 Cal. 4th 198. Court overruled all objections. Court denied Intervenor's joinder in plaintiff's opposition to motion. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Defendant The Howard Company, Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE HOWARD COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE ISSUES.
APPEARANCES: Tad S. Shapiro, Esq. of Shapiro, Galvin, Shapiro & Moran 707/544-5858 for pltf & opposing party; Ramsey F. Kawar, Esq. of Low, Ball & Lynch 981-6630 for defts AEG LIVE, LLC AND ANSCHUTZ 12K COMPANY; Jill K. Rizzo, Esq. of LeClairRyan 913-4901 for deft & moving party The Howard Company; David W. Hughes, Esq. of Laughlin Falbo Levy & Moresi LLP 510/622-5332 for Intervenor American Home Assurance Company.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for summary judgment as to defendant The Howard Company is denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether Dwight acted as Howard's agent/employee and breached his duty to provide a safe environment. Court sustained objections numbers 5, 6 and 7 as to the Jasinski declaration but overruled all the remaining objections as to both sides. Court denied Intervenor's joinder in plaintiff's opposition to motion. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
